subject: Physics
title: Decoherence Explained
description: A fast breakdown of Decoherence Explained for builders.

hook.text: Why don't we see cats that are alive and dead? Decoherence explains where quantum weirdness hides.

buildup.text: Quantum superpositions are fragile. Any interaction with the environment leaks information outward.

discovery.text: As a system interacts with trillions of particles, its quantum behavior blurs into classical behavior.

twist.text: Decoherence doesn't solve the measurement problem. It explains why we don't see weirdness, not why outcomes pick.

climax.text: Quantum computers must fight decoherence constantly. One stray photon can ruin a calculation.

punchline.text: Quantum weirdness doesn't vanish. It just leaks away into everything.

quiz.question: What does decoherence explain?
options: Why quantum effects are invisible at large scales
